note: Difficulty-tiered. easy/recipe (response-graded, no AI provider/API key needed) probes ai_agents-specific architecture facts an unfamiliar agent would have to read source to answer. medium/introspection asks about the CURRENT live setup: a setup script saves a KNOWN ai_agent config entity (eval_known_agent) and the model must inspect the running site with drush to read its tools, system prompt, and max_loops back; a cleanup script deletes it after. hard/execution requires the model to actually BUILD a working, tool-enabled AI agent on the live site (verified against site state), not just answer questions.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal ai_agents module, what is the plugin type (and its Plugin/ namespace) for writing a code-based agent? Name it.
evals.expected_output: The AiAgent plugin type (namespace Plugin/AiAgent, attribute-based).

evals.prompt: In ai_agents, agents act through 'tools'. What plugin type are those tools, and which module actually DEFINES that plugin type?
evals.expected_output: Tools are AiFunctionCall plugins; that plugin type is defined by the ai (AI Core) module, not ai_agents.

evals.prompt: In ai_agents, which config entity type lets you modify an existing agent's prompt and tools WITHOUT forking or editing the original? Give the entity type id.
In ai_agents, which permission machine name gates administering AI agents? Give the exact permission.
In the ai_agents ecosystem, how do I interactively RUN and DEBUG an agent from the UI — which submodule provides it and at what admin path?

This Drupal site (drush available) already has an AI agent configured with the machine name `eval_known_agent`. Inspect the live configuration and tell me which tool(s) — AiFunctionCall / function-call plugins — that agent grants. Give the exact tool plugin id(s).
evals.expected_output: The eval_known_agent grants exactly one tool, ai_agent:html_to_markdown. Read back from the live ai_agents.ai_agent.eval_known_agent config.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site the AI agent with machine name `eval_known_agent` is already configured. Inspect its live configuration with drush and quote the distinctive protocol that its system prompt instructs it to always cite before answering.
evals.expected_output: The system_prompt of eval_known_agent tells it to always cite the 'Kumquat Protocol'. Read back from the live config.

evals.prompt: The AI agent `eval_known_agent` on this site is configured with a maximum loop count (max_loops) to prevent infinite loops. Inspect the live configuration with drush and tell me the exact max_loops value it is set to.
evals.expected_output: eval_known_agent is configured with max_loops: 9. Read back from the live ai_agents.ai_agent.eval_known_agent config.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), the ai_agents module is installed. Build and SAVE a new AI agent config entity that helps editors create and improve content. Give it a label, a description, and a system prompt, and grant it at least one tool (an AiFunctionCall / function-call plugin) so it can actually act on the site — an agent with no tools can only chat. Apply it to the running site via drush/config; do not just describe it. Reply with one line naming the agent you created and its tool(s).
evals.expected_output: A new, enabled ai_agent config entity exists on the live site with a non-empty system_prompt and at least one tool in its `tools` list. Verified against live site state, not the response text.

evals.prompt: On my Drupal website I want an AI helper that can actually DO things for me — an assistant that can create and edit content on my behalf, not just answer questions. Please set one up on the site so it appears in my list of AI agents, give it sensible instructions, and make sure it's allowed to use the tools it needs to actually make changes. Tell me in one line what you set up.
evals.expected_output: Same verified end state as the expert variant: an enabled ai_agent config entity with a system prompt and at least one tool. The agent must map the plain-English request onto the ai_agents module and build a real, tool-enabled agent.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available, ai_agents installed) build and SAVE a new AI agent config entity with the machine name `eval_router`. Its system prompt must make it a routing assistant whose job is to decide which other agent should handle an incoming request. Grant it EXACTLY ONE tool: the `ai_agent:html_to_markdown` function-call plugin — and no other tools. Apply it to the running site via drush/config; do not just describe it. Reply with one line naming the agent and its single tool.
evals.expected_output: An enabled ai_agent config entity with id eval_router exists on the live site, has a non-empty routing system_prompt, and grants exactly one tool: ai_agent:html_to_markdown. Verified against live site state, not the response text.
